turns.student: Before another problem, can we keep the example-first thing? It helps me settle in before the rule talk.
I can explain the chain rule now: outer derivative/inner derivative means take the outer derivative, leave the inside alone, times inner derivative.
For sin(5x^2), I think the outside derivative is cos(5x^2), and then I multiply by 10x for the inside. Is that the full move?
Also, the shorter late-night practice sets worked better than the long worksheet. I actually did them after work.
I still want one small question at a time, but the chain-rule panic is way lower now.
